title: MetricRecorded payload
description: Emitted during a benchmark run when a per-step rubric judgment lands. One MetricRecorded per scored step; refs.caused_by_event_id always points at the BenchmarkRunStarted for the run, so the span tree reconstructs the run end-to-end. This is the closing of audit §4.5 item (3) — intermediate agent decisions during a run are logged.

run_id.description: The run_id from BenchmarkRunStarted that this metric belongs to.

step_id.description: Identifier for the specific step within the run that this metric scored. E.g. 'read_task', 'complete_task', 'rubric_judge_step_3'.

metric_name.description: Name of the metric being recorded. Matches the rubric's metric definitions (e.g. 'invocation_ok', 'sequence_correct', 'tool_use_efficiency').

metric_value.description: The metric's value. Permissive type — booleans for pass/fail metrics, numbers for scores, strings for categorical (e.g. 'fast' / 'slow' / 'fail'). Per-metric type discipline lives in the rubric definition, not this schema.

rationale.description: Per-metric rationale from the rubric judge (e.g. 'agent used the wrong tool but recovered gracefully'). Distinct from envelope.rationale, which on cascade-emitted MetricRecorded events inherits from the BenchmarkRunStarted's agent context. This field is the rubric judge's reasoning for THIS metric specifically.
